observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
3. direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical count on
all PPE items; prepare a duly accomplished RPCPPE and submit one copy to the
Audit Team and one copy to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
4. direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated property cards per category
on all property, plant and and reconcile with the property ledger cards maintained
by the Municipal Accountant.
Also, in view of the materiality of the amount, the conduct of a complete physical
count must be given priority.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. create an Inventory Committee and require the conduct of physical
inventory on all the local roads under its jurisdiction, prepare and submit a
verified Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network Systems
(RPCRNS);
b. direct the General Services Officer, the Municipal Engineer and the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records;
c. require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update subsidiary
ledgers, and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the local road
network system as prescribed; and
d. require all officers concerned to comply with the accounting and reporting
guidelines on the Local Road Asset Management System prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23,2015.

a. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks
at the end of the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant;
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
2. Prepare the JEV to restore the amount equivalent to the unclaimed
checks to the CIB account and likewise recognize the proper
payable/liability account. In the ensuing year, a JEV shall be prepared
to reverse the entry made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee, to conduct complete physical count of inventories,
prepare the RPCI and to reconcile with the property and accounting
records;
b. Weekly submission of RIS for consolidation and recording in property cards
and submission of SSMI to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of
the issuance and distribution;
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV for the recording of the appropriate
inventory expense based on the SSMI submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer in order to present the actual balance of Inventories for
Distribution in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor optimally utilize the 20% DF
allocated for the implementation of the approved PPAs that are envisioned to
help in the attainment of its social, economic and environmental development.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen its
disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with Source
Agencies to provide MOA or its equivalent in order to provide the pertinent
information as to the utilization of the fund. It is further recommended to
require the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to remit all collections due the National Government
Agencies to reduce the obligations or liabilities of the Municipality; and
b) Municipal Accountant to initiate preparation of final/terminal reports of
Fund Utilization and return the unutilized balances to the SAs.

In order to establish the regularity of the grant of the AHDP and SRA, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit the lacking documents for the proper disposition in the audit of the
disbursement. Henceforth, only those disbursement vouchers/payrolls with
complete supporting documents should be processed for payment

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following
journal entries to adjust the balance of the affected liability accounts:
Account Code Account Title Debit Credit
1-01-01-010 Due to LGUs P100,000.00
2-03-01-010 Due to Other Funds 300,000.00
Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk
P400,000.00
2-04-01-020 Reduction and Management
Fund

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare a request for write-off to the Commission on Audit, following the
prescribed documentations and procedures in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prepare journal entry voucher to record the provision of allowance for
impairment loss for the non-collection and non-liquidation, to fairly
present the Other Receivables-Due from NGOs/POs account in the
Financial Statements; and
b. Make a request for write-off to the Commission on Audit with all the
required documents following the prescribed procedures in COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to henceforth,
monitor, review and verify the reciprocal accounts from the subsidiary ledgers
and source documents and effect the appropriate adjustments to correct the
account balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly utilize the results of the net physical count on PPE in reconciling all maintained records of the accounting office and the GSO. Furthermore, in order to effectively undertake the reconciliation, the measures proposed in the previous year is to be followed/ that they conduct the physical count.
observations.reason: Did not conduct Physical Count of PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records on PPE and conduct physical count of land, buildings and other structures and other properties of the LGU. Moreover, specific courses of actions shall be carried out as follows, among others: a) In the absence of GSO, designate property custodian in the treasurer’s office to take custody over all properties and who will be responsible for the maintenance of PPE cards; b) In the absence of an adequate spaced stockroom for supplies, designate supplies custodian for every office who shall take custody over all inventories and the maintenance of stock cards; c) Create a committee who will conduct physical inventory taking regularly for all inventories as well as PPE items; and d) The Municipal Accountant shall maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for inventories and PPE items. All records shall be updated and reconciled regularly with issuances of inventories and PPE items duly supported with Requisition Issue slips and Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE).

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor that a written guideline is issued prior to
every inventory taking, with clear instructions on the schedule of inventory-taking;
roles of each member of the Inventory Committee; date of preparation and
submission of RPCPPE to the Audit Team, in order to ensure that a timely
completion of physical count is undertaken, and, that a Report on the Physical
Count of PPE is prepared, reconciled with the records of the accounting office, and
submitted to the Audit Team for validation.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory Team to
expedite the conduct of physical count of PPE, and, the conduct of the
reconciliation process between the RPCPPE to the PPELC maintained by the
Municipal Accountant, so that details of the beginning balances can be
provided.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to record the checks that had become stale in order to adjust the Cash in
Bank and reflect the correct amount in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to discontinue the practice of classifying as PPE, tangible items with estimated
useful life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of
the LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and
employees concerned to settle their cash advance.
b. require the Municipal Treasurer the withholding of salaries and claims of
officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the
prescribed period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No.
97-002 after being sent notice and demand.
c. require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and
obligation requests of officials and employees with outstanding cash
advances.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. prepare journal entry voucher to record the provision of allowance for
impairment loss for the non-collection and non-liquidation, to fairly present
the Other Receivables-Due From NGOs/POs account in the Financial
Statements; and,
b. make a request for write-off to COA with all the pertinent documents.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant:
a) to exert all effort to locate the documents to support this account;
b) to send demand letters with the signature of the Municipal Mayor to those
employees who have not yet settled their disallowances, and,
c) to strictly monitor the settlement of suspension and disallowances.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to maintain a special account in the General Fund for all loans availed of by
the municipality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the incumbent Municipal
Treasurer to submit to COA the missing documents to facilitate post-audit of
accounts and establish accountabilities.
We likewise recommended to the Municipal Mayor to ensure the proper turn-
over of accountability between the outgoing and incoming collecting officer for
the smooth transition of accountability.

a. direct Mr. Tirso A. Rico, Jr. to locate all missing disbursement vouchers
and its supporting documents, as well as duplicate copies of issued
official receipts of the Revenue Collectors and submit the same
immediately to the Municipal Accountant.
b. require Mr. Rico, Jr., to submit explanation why these documents were not
turned-over to the Municipal Accountant for recording and for submission
to the Audit Team for post-audit;
c. require the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents in question to
the Audit Team for post-audit

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the
existence of the fund transfers
b. reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and the
accounting records, and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling
items noted, and require liquidation of the balances.

We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Officials concerned in the implementation of projects funded by NGAs to:
a. conduct thorough planning of livelihood projects, programs and activities
and consider their viability like ensuring that suppliers of proposed project
are available in the Municipality and skills trainers are available;
b. properly monitor the implementation of the projects funded from funds
transferred from the National Government Agencies to ensure compliance
with the Memorandum of Agreement;
c. require end users to inspect items delivered together with Municipal
personnel so discrepancies could be detected.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the
existence of the fund transfers
b. reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and the
accounting records, and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling
items noted, and require liquidation of the balances.

We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Officials concerned in the implementation of projects funded by NGAs to:
a. conduct thorough planning of livelihood projects, programs and activities
and consider their viability like ensuring that suppliers of proposed project
are available in the Municipality and skills trainers are available;
b. properly monitor the implementation of the projects funded from funds
transferred from the National Government Agencies to ensure compliance
with the Memorandum of Agreement;
c. require end users to inspect items delivered together with Municipal
personnel so discrepancies could be detected.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the required lacking documents mentioned in Table 8,
and henceforth, ensure that disbursements be supported with the required
documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the BAC to fast track
the bidding process and shall be on a “jury duty” until the Notice of Award is
issued by the HOPE in order to complete the entire procurement process at
the earliest possible time.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to :
a) require the LDRRMC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of
projects/programs and activities as embodied in the LDRRMFIP;
b) appoint a MDRRM Officer to focus on the PPA implementation of the
DRRM of the Municipality and to implement planned
programs/projects/activities in order to improve the capacity of the
Municipality to respond to emergencies or disasters;

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the Local Sanggunian to:
1. craft ordinances and issue executive orders related to waste
management and environmental protection;
2. continuously improve the current waste management system of the
Municipality, including, garbage classification, collection, and recycling;
3. add more waste recycling facilities; and,
4. focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer
to implement pertinent provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code on
revenue collection so that revenue targets would be achieved to fund various
programs and projects of the Municipality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the GAD Focal Person
to conduct gender analysis in the formulation of programs and projects and
activities so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services,
protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant to adapt a monitoring mechanism to detect
variances in the amount to be remitted and withheld. Furthermore, we also
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit all financial transaction
documents such as the disbursement vouchers, payrolls and official receipts
to the accounting section on or before the 5th day of the month so that
accounting records such as check disbursement journal, cash disbursement
journal, journal of collections and deposits, general journals, general and
subsidiary ledgers could be updated monthly to afford the accounting section
to review and reconcile the exact amount to be remitted to the Bureau of
Internal Revenue.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require:
a) the Municipal Treasurer to fully remit the PhilHEALTH contributions
withheld;
b) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to adapt a monitoring
mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be remitted and withheld;
and
c) the Municipal Accountant to regularly update accounting records such as
cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash disbursement
journal, general and subsidiary ledgers in order to determine the exact
amount to be remitted to the PHIC.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to
promptly turn-over documents, reports and journals under its responsibility on
the prescribed time which shall be duly covered by transmittal letters to the
Accountant, who, in turn, submit the same for audit to the Audit Team.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health
Officer to facilitate the licensing of its ambulance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor-designate has no records and knowledge on Titles since she assumed office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to secure from the depository banks the outstanding checks; and b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record/adjust the Cash In Bank upon receipt of the stale/dishonored checks to correct the Cash In Bank balance.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer complied and the stale/dishonored checks were addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of Real Property Titles since she assumed office. We recommended that before any construction is made, land ownership thru a certificate of title should be obtained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct concerned Municipal officials to optimize the utilization of the 20% DF through proper planning of projects including timing of execution and coordination among offices of the Municipality; b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the project implementation to ensure its completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat: a. to submit to the Office of the Auditor In Charge all contracts with complete supporting documents within five working days from signing thereof for review; and b. to monitor compliance by the BAC Secretariat with the said requirement and their submission to the Office of the Auditor In Charge.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat: a. to observe strict compliance with the procurement process and the documentation of each procurement phase to ensure availability of complete documents after contract signing to preclude the deficiencies noted; and b. to ensure completeness of the documentation of the procurement process and the submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor In charge for review.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: a) ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of construction have been addressed and that a certification stating such be executed by the Municipal Engineer prior to the start of procurement activities to ensure that necessary arrangements and documents have been done for the project to be completed and utilized as planned; b) review thoroughly the request for work suspensions by contractors based on complete and relevant documents that will enable the assessment of the reasonableness of the reasons cited, and, have the same, support the request, and notice of suspension of work; c) specify in the notice of suspension of work the period within which work is suspended and the reasons for granting the request to facilitate monitoring; d) ensure the timely issuance of the notice of resumption of work, so work is started by the contractors immediately after the period of work suspension; and e) maintain complete and orderly files of contract implementation documents such as the notices of suspension of work and resumption of work to facilitate review and monitoring, and to ensure that appropriate actions are taken on time. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • initiate the forfeiture of the Performance Security of erring contractors in favor of the Municipality; • require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that liquidated damages will be effected on every day of delay of the completion of the project; and • require the Bids and Awards Committee to initiate the sanction of blacklisting, if warranted, of concerned erring contractors. We also recommended that Management plan properly the procurement of infrastructure projects to ensure their completion and use within the planned period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: 1. Ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with complete documentation with original copies attached to the disbursement voucher; and 2. Adhere to laws and regulations regarding financial transactions.
observations.reason: BUB disbursements still are supported with incomplete documents (Part II-Audit Observation No.12)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to fully implement the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the MDRRM Fund would be utilized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Cause the immediate repair of the garbage compactor; or b. Direct the SWM Focal Person to devise a way such that the existing dump truck complies with the requirements of Section 24 of RA No. 9003 on the use of prescribed vehicle in transporting solid wastes.
observations.reason: The transportation equipment for garbage collection is beyond repair.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management to adhere to the provisions set forth in Section 77 of R.A. No 7160.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to train persons manning the VAW Help Desks to capacitate them to handle VAW cases in a gender-responsive manner.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce immediate settlement of audit disallowances and charges. Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular 2009-006 that the settlement of disallowances and charges is made within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: But still other supporting documents were not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer: a. to ensure that market stall holders/occupants are covered with lease contract agreements in accordance with Section 25 of the Market Ordinance on the signing of contract of lease for every stall awarded; and b. to ensure that lessees observe the terms and conditions set forth therein.
observations.reason: Market Tiendas near the Baywalk is no longer rented/leased because it is due for repair.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Market Administrator: 1. to ensure that market stall holders pay their rentals regularly; 2. to ensure that delinquent stallholders be made to pay for penalties due for late payments; and 3. to ensure that vacant stalls will be bid out to interested tenants or merchants.
observations.reason: Some stall holders with previous years balance are slowly paying it off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the computation of the share of each barangay from real property tax collections to ensure the timely distribution thereof pursuant to Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.reason: Distribution of Real Property Tax to every barangay is done quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) In the absence of GSO, designate property custodian in the treasurer’s office to take custody over all properties and who will be responsible for the maintenance of PPE cards; b) In the absence of an adequate spaced stockroom for supplies, designate supplies custodian for every office who shall take custody over all inventories and the maintenance of stock cards; c) Create a committee who will conduct physical inventory taking regularly for all inventories as well as PPE items; and d) The Municipal Accountant shall maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for inventories and PPE items. All records shall be updated and reconciled regularly with issuances of inventories and PPE items duly supported with Requisition Issue slips and Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE).

We recommended the Local Chief Executive: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder; b) Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after being sent notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to delete the name of the official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular 96-004; c) Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and obligation requests of officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: But Cash Advances pertaining to prior years amounting to P759,082.79 remained unliquidated due to absence of subsidiary ledgers or list, hence, were not settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Education fund as mandated in Sections 272 and 100 © Local School Board implement and utilize the proceeds of the Special Education Fund as planned and mandated in the SEF.

We recommended: 1. Direct the Municipal engineer and/or the BAC Chairman to see to it that copy of contract of the project shall be attached to every billings made by the contractor; 2. Require the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of documents of every transactions before claims are to be paid; and 3. Compel all other concerned personnel involve in fiscal administration to adhere faithfully fundamental principles of financial transactions to prevent any suspension and disallowance during post-audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel all heads of offices concerned to fast track the implementation of various projects funded from fund transfers of National Government Agencies to facilitate the liquidation of funds as well as the availment of benefits of projects by the constituents as envisioned by the Philippine Development Plan.

We recommended that the AIP for Solid Waste Management of the Municipality must be planned carefully and priority programs, activities and projects shall be implemented for the achievement of the goals of Solid Waste Management of the LGU as mandated by Republic Act 9003.

We recommended: 1. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor implementation of the 20% Development Fund. 2. Direct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the preparation of engineering plans, designs, programs of work, etc. and required documents; 3. Direct other support staff and personnel to facilitate completion of documents relative to programs and projects funded by the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local chief Executive: 1. Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 8291 so that failures and lapses will be avoided. 2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding was accrued. 3. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation with its records with that of the GSIS and give priority to those affected employees with negative balances for one (1) month to three (3) years’ and request from GSIS for refund.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the full liquidation of all cash advances to preclude their suspension and disallowance during audit.
observations.reason: But Cash Advances amounting to P759,082.79 remained unliquidated due to absence of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency comply with all the requirement of RA 9184, its implementing rules and all laws, decrees, circulars, memoranda and issuances governing infrastructure projects of local governments. Specifically, we recommend that: a) A certification that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications in conformance with Annex A of IRR of RA 9184 should be attached in the contract and that the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand. b) The contracts for infrastructure projects did not provide subsequent provisions for the process of arbitration in case of disagreement between the parties in accordance with the requirement of Section 59.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. The contracts for infrastructure projects should be reviewed and revised to include the required provision so that contracts which will be entered into by the city government would comply with the relevant requirements of RA 9184 IRR and related laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the prompt implementation of BUB Programs/Projects/Activities within the timeframe set by the Source Agencies to address the poverty reduction objective of the program.
observations.reason: BUB programs were implemented but delays remained.

observations.managementAction: The management is having a hard time on the procurement process of some of the projects. They are doing their best to implement the projects and required documents.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee
headed by the Municipal Treasurer to conduct complete physical inventory
count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE). We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
RPCPPE with accounting regularly reconcile their records and review the
composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant
and Equipment account balance.

1.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock and Property Cards for
Supplies, and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively. The
required records shall also be the basis by the GSO for its reconciliation with
the actual physical inventory.

2.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to secure from the depository banks the
foregoing outstanding checks; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record/adjust the Cash In Bank upon
receipt of the stale/dishonored checks to correct the Cash In Bank balance.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the
Municipality. We further recommended that before any construction is made,
land ownership thru a certificate of title should be obtained.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal officials concerned to optimize the utilization of the
20% DF through proper planning of projects including timing of execution
and coordination among offices of the Municipality.
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the project
implementation to ensure its completion.

5.8 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat:
a. to submit to the Office of the Auditor In Charge all contracts with complete
supporting documents within five working days from signing thereof for
review; and
b. to monitor compliance by the BAC Secretariat with the said requirement and
their submission to the Office of the Auditor In Charge.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat:
a. to observe strict compliance with the procurement process and the
documentation of each procurement phase to ensure availability of complete
documents after contract signing to preclude the deficiencies noted; and
b. to ensure completeness of the documentation of the procurement process
and the submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor In charge for
review.

7.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to:
 ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of construction have
been addressed and that a certification stating such be executed by the Municipal
Engineer prior to the start of procurement activities to ensure that necessary
arrangements and documents have been done for the project to be completed and
utilized as planned;
 review thoroughly the request for work suspensions by contractors based on
complete and relevant documents that will enable the assessment of the
reasonableness of the reasons cited, and, have the same, support the request, and
notice of suspension of work;
 specify in the notice of suspension of work the period within which work is
suspended and the reasons for granting the request to facilitate monitoring;
 ensure the timely issuance of the notice of resumption of work, so work is started
by the contractors immediately after the period of work suspension; and
 maintain complete and orderly files of contract implementation documents such
as the notices of suspension of work and resumption of work to facilitate review
and monitoring, and to ensure that appropriate actions are taken on time.

7.12 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Initiate the forfeiture of the Performance Security of erring contractors in favor of
the Municipality;
 Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that
liquidated damages will be effected on every day of delay of the completion of the
project; and
 Require the Bids and Awards Committee to initiate the sanction of
blacklisting, if warranted, of concerned erring contractors.

7.13 We also recommended that Management plan properly the procurement of
infrastructure projects to ensure their completion and use within the planned
period.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with
complete documentation with original copies attached to the disbursement
voucher;
b. Adhere to laws and regulations regarding financial transactions.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Officer to fully implement the planned
programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response,
rehabilitation and recovery so that the MDRRM Fund would be utilized and the
intended benefits would be fully achieved.

10.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Cause the immediate repair of the garbage compactor; or
b. Direct the SWM Focal Person to devise a way such that the existing dump
truck complies with the requirements of Section 24 of RA No. 9003 on the
use of prescribed vehicle in transporting solid wastes.

11.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource
Management Officer to adhere to the provisions set forth in Section 77 of R.A.
No 7160.

12.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

13.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
train persons manning the VAW Help Desks to capacitate them to handle VAW
cases in a gender-responsive manner.

14.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce immediate settlement of the
audit disallowances and charges. Likewise, ensure that all employees who are
retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and
charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular
2009-006 that the settlement of disallowances and charges is made within the
prescribed period.

15.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer:
a. to ensure that market stall holders/occupants are covered with lease contract
agreements in accordance with Section 25 of the Market Ordinance on the
signing of contract of lease for every stall awarded: and
b. to ensure that lessees observe the terms and conditions set forth therein.

16.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer
thru the Market Administrator:
1. to ensure that market stall holders pay their rentals regularly;
2. to ensure that delinquent stallholders be made to pay for penalties due for
late payments; and
3. to ensure that vacant stalls will be bid out to interested tenants or
merchants.

17.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the computation of the
share of each barangay from real property tax collections to ensure the timely
distribution thereof pursuant to Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160.

observations.managementAction: Taken and implemented. This was taken up in the books and actual transfer of funds made last April 2008, per Cash Receipts Journal of MDRRM Fund.
Implemented. We will incorporate the replenishment to MDRRM Fund from the General Fund in the 2019 Annual Budget.
Implemented. Municipal Accountant is maintaining separate ledgers for the loans availed by the Local Government Unit with the Landbank of the Philippines.

Implemented. Liquidation was settled by the officers who availed the advances; the Municipal Accountant will make an adjustment in the books and effect the return of the amount equivalent to the registration fee.

Implemented. Driver Trip Tickets are now prepared serially and attached to the vouchers. Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption duly supported with approved Driver's Trip Tickets are prepared and submitted to the Auditor.
Partially Implemented. The LGU allocated funds to top priority projects to effectively utilize the fund.

Implemented. The LGU is a recipient of Presidential Award for Child-Friendly and National Finalist. The Local Chief Executive caused the Local Council for Protection of Children to prepare its Annual Work and Financial Plan.
Partially Implemented. Payments were processed; implementing offices are doing their best to implement the projects. If demand for return of funds arises, LGU is willing to comply.
Partially Implemented. The budget of Solid Waste Management was funded in the 20% EDF and programs were realigned to priority ones per RA 9003.
Partially Implemented. The projects funded by the 20% Development Fund are now on completion stage.
Implemented. The current Job Orders for the current year are now adjusted as per recommendation of the auditor.
Implemented. Database established to support GAD-responsive planning and programming.
Implemented. Findings were positively taken and implemented; DTR procedures adjusted and employees instructed to use prescribed CSC forms.
Partially Implemented. Municipal Treasurer remitted withheld amounts; however some reconciliation with GSIS ongoing. No demand letter from GSIS received yet.
Implemented. Internal Audit Services organized and Internal Audit Service Officer designated.
Implemented. Municipal Accountant coordinated with Municipal Treasurer to regularize remittances; adjustments made in January 2018 General Journal.
Partially Implemented. Demand was made to the past Municipal Accountant for submission of subsidiary ledgers; some advances settled.
Partially Implemented. The LGU is addressing requirements; some certifications and detailed engineering outputs still pending.
Partially Implemented. The Inventory team did not produce the prescribed form of the repost. Thus, balance of PPE per books is still not reconciled with the inventory report.
Partially Implemented. The implementing agencies encountered challenges, particularly on procurement processes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to undertake measures in managing and
validating all recorded balances for the inventory accounts as well as PPE items per
the agency’ books of accounts thru the regular conduct of physical count and
maintenance of prescribed records. Moreover, specific courses of actions shall be
carried out as follows, among others: a) in the absence of GSO, designate property
custodian in the treasurer’s office to take custody over all properties and who will be
responsible for the maintenance of PPE cards; b) in the absence of an adequate
spaced stockroom for supplies, designate supplies custodian for every office who
shall take custody over all inventories and the maintenance of stock cards; c) create
a committee who will conduct physical inventory taking regularly for all inventories
as well as PPE items; and d) the Municipal Accountant shall maintain complete and
updated subsidiary ledgers for inventories and PPE items. All records shall be
updated and reconciled regularly with issuances of inventories and PPE items duly
supported with Requisition Issue Slips and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment
(ARE).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to
make corresponding adjustments to correct the affected accounts of LDRRMF in the
General and the LDRRM Funds so that the accounts will be presented fairly in the
Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE
or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned
to settle their cash advance.
b)Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees
who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to
Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after being sent notice and
demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to delete the name of the
official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll until such time that the cash
advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular 96-004.
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget
Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and obligation requests of
officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct concerned officials
to follow strictly the provisions embodied in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-
1 as a guide for the proper utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to comply with the prescribed regulation on the maintenance of special
account for all loan proceeds from which financial reports shall be prepared.

Based on the provision in the liquidation of cash advances as stated above,
we recommended that the Local Chief Executive Officer direct the Municipal
Accountant to produce the supporting documents of the liquidation of the
aforementioned advances for officers and employees and henceforth adhere to the
provisions of Paragraph 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on liquidation of cash
advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to compel the Driver to prepare a properly filled-up
Driver’s Trip Ticket days before the scheduled official travel and prepare
Monthly Report of Official Travels duly approved by the Municipal Treasurer
and submit to the Auditor five days after the end of each month;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption duly supported with approved and properly accomplished Driver’s
Trip Tickets and approved by him (LCE) and submit to the Auditor for audit five
days after end of each month;
c. Compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the practice of checking as
to completeness of documentations before claims are to be paid. Said measure
must be carried out to prevent the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance
during post-audit.

We recommended that the Local School Board implement and utilize the
proceeds of the Special Education Fund as planned and mandated in Sections 272
and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Budget Officer to
strictly adhere the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 known as Juvenile
Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and appropriate the required allocation of 1% of its
IRA for the LCPC activities. Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive should cause the
Local Council for the Protection of Children to prepare its Annual Work and Financial
Plan for the attainment of the LCPC projects and activities for the welfare of the
children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Engineer and/or the BAC Chairman to see to it that copy of
approved contract documents of the project shall be attached to every billings
made by the contractor;
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of documents of
every transactions before claims are to be paid; and
3. Compel all other concerned personnel involve in fiscal administration to adhere
faithfully fundamental principles of financial transactions to prevent any
suspension and disallowance during post-audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel all heads of offices
concerned to fast track the implementation of various projects funded from fund
transfers of National Government Agencies to facilitate the liquidation of the funds as
well as the availment of the benefits of the projects by the constituents as envisioned
by the Philippine Development Plan.

We recommended that the AIP for Solid Waste Management of the
Municipality must be planned carefully and priority programs, activities and projects
shall be implemented for the achievement of the goals of Solid Waste Management
of the LGU as mandated by Republic Act 9003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
implement and monitor implementation of programs and projects funded
out of the 20% Development Fund.
2. Direct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the preparation of
engineering plans, designs, programs of work, etc. and required
documents;
3. Direct other support staff and personnel to facilitate completion of
documents relative to programs and projects funded by the 20%
Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to review the duties
and functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the
duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees;
b.Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order
Workers in compliance to regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD council
together with the LPDO to come-up with the database in attuning to the demands of
GAD issues and concerns.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct all employees to use the DTR prescribed by the Civil Service
Commission “CSC Form 48” when making daily time record;
b) Require all employees to note on the date of the DTR if on official travel, on
leave, attending trainings and seminars, etc.
c) Direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) or his equivalent
to monitor the under times made by the employees and make corresponding
deductions on said under times in their leave credit balance or deduct Leaves
Without Pay in the payroll;
d) Direct the Department Heads to see to it that the employee will make time in
and time out during arrival and departure from office every morning and
afternoon and require absent employees an Approved Leave of Absence
Application;
e) Require LGU employees to file leave application when they will be absent in
accordance with Civil Service rules.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA
8291 so that failures and lapses will be avoided.
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to
GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding
was made or the withholding was accrued.
3. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct
thorough reconciliation with its records with that of the GSIS and give priority
100
to those affected employees with negative balances for one (1) month to three
(3) years, and request from GSIS for refund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should consider creating
and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO
70 to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the
operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees
with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its
obligations. The Municipal Accountant should also regularly reconcile the
amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and
reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Human
Resource Officer to device a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of
employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the
prescribed period.

We reiterated our AAR recommendation 2015 that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly utilize the results of the net of physical count on PPE in reconciling the agency’s financial records as of year-end and the all maintained accounting office and the GSO. Furthermore, in order to effectively undertake the reconciliation, implementation of the measures proposed in the previous year is to be followed.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the requisitioning officer of the medicines to certify that the medicines to be procured are compliant to the PNDF provisions.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant implement the provisions of Section 105 of NGAS to maintain separate books of accounts for each of the three economic enterprises of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy between balance per books reflected in the
financial statements and balance per books in the statement of bank reconciliation and
prepare necessary journal entries to record the reconciling items and to reflect the
correct amount for the Cash and Cash Equivalent account based on Bank
Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive of the LGU direct the
Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to conduct monthly reconciliation of records to
correct discrepancies in both records and thus reflect a correct balance of the account
in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the Report of Cash Disbursements, Liquidation Reports on Cash
Advances granted for payrolls and the corresponding paid payrolls to the accounting
section on a regular basis in order for the Municipal Accountant to update the general
ledger balances monthly and prepare the monthly cash disbursement Journal, and also
to be able to determine the correct balances of cash disbursing-payroll account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the officials concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of
Section 111 of PD 1445, and Section 491 of the GAAM Vol. I, in order to
adequately furnish the accurate information of the PPE of the LGU.
2. Require the General Supply Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to indicate the
unit value of the items in the RPCPPE and reconcile the difference of
P80,811,962.88 between the balances per books with the physical count as
of December 31, 2016 in order to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE.
3. Require the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to use the RPCPPE form with
complete signatures of the inventory team with the approval of the LCE.
4. Require both the Accounting Office and Property Officer to reclassify
tangible assets if found during the reconciliation with serviceable life of
more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE, thus Small
tangible items with estimated useful life of more than one year shall be
recorded as inventories upon acquisition and as expense upon issuance,
per Items 2.2 of COA Circular 2005-002 dated April 14, 2005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require officials
concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 80-124 dated
January 18, 1980 and Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 to strengthen the controls
currently adopted by the LGU over properties and equipment and the
recording/valuation of assets to avoid overstatement/understatement of asset cost.

We recommended that whenever medicines are procured, management should
strictly adhere to EO No. 49, DOH AO No. 163 series of 2002 and COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that items procured originated from
legitimate sources and that they are effective as well as genuine.

We also recommended that if the drugs to be requisitioned/procured are not
included in the PNDF such should be supported with a written approval of the head of
National Drug Policy Staff.

In addition, we recommended that the Property Custodian should submit to
the Accountant Monthly Report of Medicines Issued and Report of Physical
Inventory of Drugs and Medicines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local
School Board strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and advise the
school administrators in their respective locality to revise their School Improvement
Plan to include the expenses enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.

We enjoined the local officials for the utilization of the unexpended balance
for disaster risk reduction and management program, project & activities and include
the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF of the previous years which
were transferred to the Special Trust Fund in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste management
system by providing financial and technical assistance for the establishment/
operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the barangays of the
Municipality.

We likewise recommended for a wide dissemination of information on source
reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the provisions of
the IRR of RA 9003

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the prompt
implementation of BUB Programs/Projects/Activities within the timeframe set by the
Source Agencies to address the poverty reduction objective of the program.

We recommended that perfected contracts together with the documents
forming part thereof by reference or incorporation shall be furnished to the Auditor of
the agency concerned within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract.

We recommended for the submission of the lacking documents and henceforth
supporting documents should be complete to facilitate audit action and to establish
validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to file an application for bond with the Bureau of Treasury at an increased
amount sufficient to cover his maximum cash accountability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Revenue Collection Officers to file an application for bond with the
Bureau of Treasury at an increased amount sufficient to cover their maximum cash
accountability.

It is also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all revenue
collectors to be bonded commensurate to their maximum cash accountability to afford
security of government funds.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the full liquidation of all cash advances to preclude their suspension and disallowance during audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers to the COA for audit purposes within the prescribed period under existing laws.
We recommended that contracts of job order workers should contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent terms which will serve as standard with which to base accomplishment.

We recommended measures to address these deficiencies: the maximum borrowing capacity should not only consider the 20% debt service ceiling but also the LGUs’ net available funds prior to contracting of loans/borrowings; subsidiary ledgers should be maintained for each loan/borrowing; LGU should conduct self-assessment of their capacity to repay the loan taking into consideration their net available funds; ensure utilization of loan proceeds is covered by appropriation ordinance; determine the most cost effective borrowing scheme; contract loans only upon proper evaluation of priority projects; and allocate sufficient funds to cover total contracted liabilities.

We recommended that the agency comply with all requirements of RA 9184, its implementing rules and all laws, decrees, circulars, memoranda and issuances governing infrastructure projects. Specifically: a) A certification that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and in conformance with the provision of Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and that the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand; b) The contracts for infrastructure projects be reviewed and revised to include provisions for arbitration in case of disagreement between the parties in accordance with the requirement of Section 59.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and that significant terms required by the law should be incorporated in the contract.
We recommended that mandatory reports in regard MDRRMF be submitted by the local accountant in accordance with established rules and laws.
We recommended the submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report as required under DILG-PCW Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Treasurer and Accountant to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that all fund transfers must have a Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the infrastructure project must be validated upon completion through conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare an inspection report based on the statement of work accomplished by the barangay to be attached to the liquidation report.

We recommended that perfected contracts together with the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation shall be furnished to the Auditor of the agency concerned within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract.

We recommend that the Municipal Government strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of the LGC, Article 363 of its Implementing Rules and Regulations and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended: a) Act on the corrections required by the National Solid Waste Management Commission on the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan; b) Invite the active participation of barangay officials in the information dissemination to their constituents on the proper segregation of the reusable or recyclable materials from the compostable waste to support the solid waste management program of the government; and c) Acquire a lot for MRF so that proper segregation of mixed waste will be made and that only residual waste shall be transferred to the disposal facility which may eventually reduce the cost of waste disposal.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive a) require the accountant to strictly follow the guidelines contained in Section 105-111 of chapter 6, Manual on NGAS for LGUs regarding the maintenance of special accounts in the General Fund; b) require the accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for special accounts in the GF for the LGUs 20% EDF in order to have adequate and reliable information as to assets liabilities and the corresponding object of expenditure and if these were in line with the guidelines set forth in the 20% EDF guidelines.
